diagrams.title: Class Structure with Constructor and Methods
diagrams.description: A class template diagram showing the blueprint of a Python class with the __init__ constructor method and regular methods, displaying their parameters and return types.
elements: __init__ method section showing 'def __init__(self, make, model):' with 'self.make = make' and 'self.model = model' assignments
Method section showing 'def start_engine(self):' returning 'Engine started'
Another method 'def drive(self, speed):' showing speed parameter
Arrow showing self parameter connects to instance variables
diagrams.purpose: Explains the structure of a Python class, distinguishing the constructor (__init__) that initializes object state from regular methods that define behavior

diagrams.title: Object Creation and Method Invocation Flow
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing the step-by-step process from defining a class to creating an object instance and calling methods on it.
elements: Arrow to __init__ execution showing 'self' becoming the new object
Arrow to object in memory showing attributes: make='Toyota', model='Camry'
Arrow showing method accessing self.make and returning result
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how constructors initialize new objects and how methods access object state through the self parameter

diagrams.title: Self Parameter Concept
diagrams.description: Visual explanation of how 'self' works as a reference to the current object instance, showing multiple objects with independent attribute values.
elements: Three Car objects in memory: car1, car2, car3
Each object shows its own make and model values
Internal view showing 'self' inside method pointing to car1 object
Demonstration that car2.start_engine() uses car2's data
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies that 'self' is a reference to the specific object the method is called on, enabling each object to maintain its own state
